g.text: LAGGING TAIL: Thread Stalls Between Phases

div.p: Why two phases? We can only CAS one memory location at a time.

ul.li: Phase 1 modifies tail->next (the node's next field)

Phase 2 modifies queue->tail (the queue's tail pointer) Helping ensures lock-freedom: If Thread A stalls, Thread B can observe the intermediate state (tail->next != NULL) and complete the tail swing. No thread can block progress indefinitely.

g.text: Before: Thread A stalls after Phase 1

div.p: Linearization Point tail->next now points to new_node Element is logically in queue
Tail now points to new_node Queue state is fully consistent Other threads see correct tail

g.text: Thread A linked node_A but didn't swing tail yet
Thread B helps complete Thread A's enqueue!

text.tspan: // PHASE 2: Swing tail (can fail - others may help)
// Another thread linked but didn't swing tail

g.text: CAS(&queue->tail, tail, next); // Advance it ourselves

text.tspan: // PHASE 2: Swing tail (can fail - others may help)
// Another thread linked but didn't swing tail
